model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The run is fixed by step count rather than epoch count, so doubling batch size lets the model see twice as many training examples within the same 256-step budget. This is a simple high-leverage change for short-budget CIFAR training and avoids adding optimizer or architecture complexity before testing the data-throughput bottleneck.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The previous step improved substantially by increasing data throughput under a fixed-step budget. Pushing batch size one more step tests whether the current bottleneck is still limited by examples seen rather than optimization noise, while keeping the code unchanged otherwise.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing batch size from 64 to 256, the optimizer is likely under-stepping relative to the larger effective batch. Raising the AdamW learning rate is a simple follow-up that tests better optimization scale without changing the model or training loop.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization settings now look much stronger than the starting baseline, so the next likely bottleneck is model capacity. Doubling the base channel width is a simple way to test whether this micro ResNet is underpowered under the improved fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent gains suggest the run is still capacity-limited, but widening further could become expensive on CPU. Adding one more residual block is a targeted depth increase that meaningfully expands representational power while keeping the current successful optimizer and batch settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The convolutional trunk has been strengthened substantially, so the small 32-unit classifier may now be the narrowest part of the network. Expanding the hidden layer is a cheap capacity increase compared with widening or deepening the convolution stack further.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"This run is still in a short-budget regime with no label noise or class imbalance, so the stronger capacity may now be more limited by regularization than by overfitting. Reducing weight decay is a simple way to test whether the current model can convert its larger architecture into lower validation loss more effectively.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger model and reduced weight decay still improved, which suggests optimization headroom remains. A moderate learning-rate increase is the simplest next test to see whether the current short-budget setup benefits from more aggressive early progress before introducing more complex scheduler changes.","secondary_modes":["caching","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last step degraded validation loss, which indicates the learning-rate increase overshot the useful range for the current model and batch configuration. Reverting to the previously better 3e-3 setting restores the strongest known optimization scale before exploring other changes.","secondary_modes":["caching","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current configuration has a larger model with minimal explicit regularization beyond weight decay, and recent schedule changes did not help. Adding modest label smoothing is a simple robustness change that often improves CIFAR generalization for AdamW-trained classifiers without altering the architecture or budget usage.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The strongest gains so far have come from increasing model capacity, and the latest label-smoothing change suggests the current regime can support a larger network. Increasing the base width again is the most direct next test of whether the run is still capacity-limited under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing helped materially, so it is worth tuning its strength rather than treating 0.1 as fixed. A smaller value keeps the regularization benefit while reducing the risk of underfitting confidence on a clean-label CIFAR-10 task.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Recent improvements have come from modest capacity increases and regularization tuning, while the classifier remains comparatively small relative to the widened convolutional trunk. Expanding the hidden layer is a cheap next capacity test that should add little runtime compared with further widening the convolution stack.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The best gains are still coming from added capacity, and the widened network plus larger classifier suggests the remaining bottleneck may be trunk depth rather than optimization. Adding one more residual block is a focused convolutional-capacity increase that preserves the currently strong optimizer, batch, and regularization settings.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),

Adding one more residual block is the most direct continuation of the strongest improving trend with only a localized architecture edit.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),

Reverting to three residual blocks restores the best-known trunk depth before using the final steps on a different axis.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.02)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing has been consistently helpful, and reducing it from 0.1 to 0.05 already improved the larger/deeper model. With the current stronger architecture, a smaller smoothing value is a reasonable final refinement to keep the regularization benefit while reducing under-confidence on this clean-label task.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}
